Breast Feeding Support | Sutter County, CA
Show/Hide
ROAD CLOSURES
COMMUNITY ACADEMY
Lactation Support
Our Lactation Specialists offer assistance to families, helping them achieve their breastfeeding goals. Services include telephone and in-person consultations, as well as home visits upon request.
For more information on the benefits of breastfeeding and how to get the support you need, please visit the provided resources, and feel free to reach out to our Lactation Specialists for personalized assistance. Your breastfeeding journey is important to us, and we're here to support you every step of the way.
Call (530) 822-7215 or 1-800-371-3177 or
via email
to talk with a Sutter County Public Health Lactation Specialists. Our specialists are available to provide support tailored to your family’s needs, including:
Telephone Consultations
: Feel free to call us with your questions and concerns.
In-Person Consultations
: Schedule an appointment with our experts for one-on-one guidance.
Home Visits
: If you prefer, our counselors can visit your home to provide personalized support.
Benefits of Breastfeeding:
Breast milk is the most complete form of nutrition for infants, offering a range of health benefits, including reducing the risk of infections, cancer, diabetes, obesity, and asthma.
It promotes bonding between the baby and mother.
It is easily digestible and tailored to meet the specific nutritional needs of your baby.
Breastfeeding can support postpartum recovery for mothers.
Top Tips for Breastfeeding Success:
Ensure Proper Latch: A correct latch is crucial for comfortable and effective breastfeeding.
Learn More
Breastfeeding positions:
Learn More
Frequent Nursing: Frequent breastfeeding sessions help establish your milk supply.
Learn More
Skin-to-Skin Contact: Holding your baby skin-to-skin can promote bonding and milk flow.
Learn More
Stay Hydrated and Eat a Healthy Diet: Drinking plenty of fluids and eating nutrient rich foods are essential for both you and your baby.
Learn More
Prioritize Rest: Adequate rest is crucial for your well-being and milk production.
Learn More
Additional Breastfeeding Support Resources:
WIC: Women, infants, & Children (WIC) Program is a federally funded health and nutrition program. A short screening process over the phone or in-person can help you find out if you qualify for the Sutter County WIC Program.
(530) 822-7224 or
Email Sutter County WIC
1445 Veterans Memorial Circle, Yuba City.
